sanskrit: योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः । धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥
hindiTranslation: दुष्टबुद्धि दुर्योधन का युद्ध में प्रिय करने की इच्छावाले जो ये राजालोग इस सेना में आये हुए हैं, युद्ध करने को उतावले हुए इन सबको मैं देख लूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I want to see those who have gathered here, eager to fight and wishing to please the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra.
essence: Conflict becomes real when you look at the people involved.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna wants to inspect the warriors gathered on the opposite side, especially those fighting for Duryodhana.
simpleInsight: Before a hard choice, he looks directly at the people involved instead of hiding from the reality.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna has asked Krishna to place the chariot between the armies. Now, in the charged silence before the battle, he wants to see the warriors who have come to support Duryodhana. This is the moment his hesitation turns into a direct look at the cost of war.
modernRelevance: You pause before sending the message that will end a friendship. First you read the names again, face the people involved, and feel the weight of what is about to happen.
translationLiteral: योत्स्यमानान् (those eager to fight) / अवेक्षे (I want to see) / अहम् (I) / ये (who) / एते (these) / अत्र (here) / समागताः (assembled) / धार्तराष्ट्रस्य (of Dhritarashtra’s son) / दुर्बुद्धेः (of the evil-minded) / युद्धे (in battle) / प्रियचिकीर्षवः (wishing to please)
